# Boston's Cursor Community

Bringing Cursor users together in Beantown. Meetups, workshops, and community for AI-powered development.

[Subscribe to Events](https://lu.ma/cursor-boston) [View Events](https://www.cursorboston.com/events)

## Featured: Hack-a-Sprint

[Hackathons & details →](https://www.cursorboston.com/hackathons) [All events →](https://www.cursorboston.com/events)

![Cursor Boston Hack-a-Sprint graphic](https://www.cursorboston.com/_next/image?url=%2Fcursor-boston-logo.png&w=3840&q=75&dpl=dpl_3AtZit4VZHRhVcrk5jYCo8yA5E2V)

In-person hackathon · 50 spots

### Cursor Boston Hack-a-Sprint

First-ever Cursor Boston Hack-a-Sprint: 50 developers, fast solo competition, fully fed and watered. $50 in Cursor Credits for every selected participant, $1,200 prize pool (six $200 spots). Monday April 13 in Back Bay — register on Luma; approval required.

When:Monday, April 13, 2026 · 4:00 PM – 8:00 PM ET

Where:Back Bay, Boston, MA. Exact address on Luma after approval.

$50 Cursor Credits each$1,200 prize poolFood & drinks

[Register for event](https://luma.com/event/evt-Wkfyzu00afkUQaX) [More details](https://www.cursorboston.com/hackathons)

## Who's This For?

Whether you're deep into your daily Cursor flow or just curious about AI-powered development, our events are for everyone.

### Students

From MIT, Harvard, Hult, BU, Northeastern, and beyond. Learn AI-powered development skills that will set you apart.

### Startup Founders

Prototype MVPs in hours, not weeks. Build landing pages, dashboards, and validate ideas without a technical co-founder.

### Developers

Ship production-ready features faster. Debug, test, and build full-stack applications with AI assistance.

### Designers & PMs

Turn designs into code. Build prototypes, automate workflows, and create professional deliverables faster.

### AI AgentsNew

Yes, agents too! Register your AI agent, claim ownership, and join our community alongside human members.

## Join Our Community

Connect with other Cursor users in Boston. Share tips, ask questions, and stay updated on upcoming events.

Growing community of developers, founders & students

[Join Discord Server](https://discord.gg/Wsncg8YYqc)

## Ready to Level Up?

Subscribe to our Luma calendar to get notified about upcoming meetups, workshops, and hackathons in Boston.

[Subscribe on Luma](https://lu.ma/cursor-boston)

## Welcome to Cursor Boston!

Join our community of developers, founders, and students exploring AI-powered development.

[Join Our Discord](https://discord.gg/Wsncg8YYqc) [View Upcoming Events](https://www.cursorboston.com/events) [Register for Event](https://luma.com/u43ar9pi)

# Events

Join us for workshops, meetups, hackathons, and more. All skill levels welcome.

[Subscribe on Luma](https://lu.ma/cursor-boston) [View Event Map](https://www.cursorboston.com/map)

## Event Types

### Workshops

### Meetups

Networking, demos, and community discussions

### Hackathons

Build projects with AI-powered development tools

### University Sessions

### Cursor Boston/AIC BTW Open Source Workshop

Open Source Drop-In Workshop co-hosted by Cursor Boston and AIC. A hands-on half-day session with two tracks: contribute to the Cursor Boston repo (beginner-friendly) or tackle curated impactful open source projects using Cursor as your AI co-pilot. Walk away with merged PRs, Cursor credits, and practical open source skills. Saturday May 30 in Boston — register on Luma; approval required.

#### Topics Covered

Open Source ContributionAI-Assisted Coding with CursorGit & GitHub WorkflowsTwo Tracks (Beginner & Advanced)Hands-On PR Workshops

Boston, MA

[View Details](https://www.cursorboston.com/events/cursor-boston-aic-open-source-workshop-2026) [Register on Luma](https://luma.com/event/evt-2erXQCohWrtPWp9)

pages.content:
[Skip to main content](https://www.cursorboston.com/talks#main-content)

# Talks

Archive of presentations from our community events. Learn how others are using Cursor in their workflows.

## Talk Categories

### Academic Writing & Research

Using AI for literature synthesis, fact-checking, and academic work

### Career & First Job Success

Excel, automation, and professional deliverables with AI

### Software Development

Full-stack development, debugging, and production code with Cursor

### Startup Building

MVPs, landing pages, and rapid prototyping for founders

## Featured Talks

### Coming Soon

Talks from our events will be added here after our first meetup. Check back soon!

## Featured Articles

Key AI research from Boston-area universities

[MIT News **Helping AI agents search to get the best results out of large language models** \\
\\
EnCompass executes AI agent programs by backtracking and making multiple attempts, finding the best set of outputs generated by an LLM.\\
\\
Read Article](https://news.mit.edu/2026/helping-ai-agents-search-to-get-best-results-from-llms-0205) [MIT News **New AI model could cut the costs of developing protein drugs** \\
\\
MIT researchers use large language models to optimize genetic sequences of proteins manufactured by yeast.\\
\\
Read Article](https://news.mit.edu/2026/new-ai-model-could-cut-costs-developing-protein-drugs-0216) [MIT News **AI-generated sensors open new paths for early cancer detection** \\
\\
MIT and Microsoft researchers developed an AI model to design molecular sensors using nanoparticles coated with AI-generated peptides.\\
\\
Read Article](https://news.mit.edu/2026/ai-generated-sensors-open-new-paths-early-cancer-detection-0106) [Harvard Gazette **When you do the math, humans still rule** \\
\\
Harvard mathematician Lauren Williams leads First Proof, challenging AI to solve unpublished research-level math problems.\\
\\
Read Article](https://news.harvard.edu/gazette/story/2026/02/when-you-do-the-math-humans-still-rule) [Harvard Gazette **New kind of AI model gives robots a visual imagination** \\
\\
Kempner Institute investigator Yilun Du unveils Large Video Planner, enabling robots to envision actions before executing them.\\
\\
Read Article](https://news.harvard.edu/gazette/story/newsplus/new-kind-of-ai-model-gives-robots-a-visual-imagination) [BU Today **Boston University Ramps Up Strategy on AI** \\
\\
BU launches the Artificial Intelligence Development Accelerator (AIDA) for university-wide AI adoption and collaboration.\\
\\
Read Article](https://www.bu.edu/articles/2025/boston-university-ramps-up-strategy-on-ai) [BU Today **BU Launches Open-Source AI Infectious Diseases Monitoring Tool** \\
\\
BEACON combines AI analysis with human expert verification to monitor infectious disease outbreaks globally.\\
\\
Read Article](https://www.bu.edu/articles/2025/open-source-ai-infectious-diseases-monitoring-tool)

## Share Your Ideas

We're always looking for speakers to share their experiences with Cursor and AI-powered development.

**All skill levels are welcome**– whether you're a seasoned developer, a designer exploring AI tools, a student learning to code, or from any other discipline using Cursor in creative ways.

### Talk ideas include:

- A workflow or technique you've discovered
- A project you built with Cursor
- Tips for using Cursor in a specific domain
- Lessons learned from AI-assisted development
- Live coding demos and tutorials

[Submit Your Talk Idea](https://www.cursorboston.com/talks/submit)

## Welcome to Cursor Boston!

Join our community of developers, founders, and students exploring AI-powered development.

[Join Our Discord](https://discord.gg/Wsncg8YYqc) [View Upcoming Events](https://www.cursorboston.com/events) [Register for Event](https://luma.com/u43ar9pi)

![Cursor Boston](https://www.cursorboston.com/_next/image?url=%2Fcursor-boston-logo.png&w=3840&q=75&dpl=dpl_3WpifsZ9mtsAJfFmEz8RarpCmtuK)

# About Cursor Boston

A community for exploring and discussing AI-powered development with Cursor, right here in Beantown.

## Our Mission

Cursor Boston brings together developers, designers, students, startup founders, and anyone curious about how AI can transform the way we build software.

We're part of the global [Cursor community](https://cursor.com/community), hosting local meetups, workshops, and hackathons in the Boston area. Whether you're deep into your daily Cursor flow or just getting started with AI-assisted coding, our events are for you.

Boston has always been a hub for innovation — from world-class universities to cutting-edge startups. We believe AI-powered development tools like Cursor are the next chapter in that story, and we're excited to help build the community around it.

## The Boston Tech Ecosystem

Boston is home to some of the world's most innovative institutions and companies. Cursor Boston connects with this vibrant ecosystem.

### Universities

#### MIT

Kendall Square innovation hub

#### Harvard

Innovation Labs & i-lab

#### Hult International Business School

Business & tech

#### Northeastern University

Co-op & tech programs

#### Boston University

Engineering & CS

#### Boston College

STEM programs

### Accelerators & Labs

#### MassChallenge

Equity-free startup accelerator

#### Techstars Boston

Mentor-driven accelerator program

#### The Engine

MIT's tough tech accelerator

#### Greentown Labs

Climate tech incubator

## Get Involved with Cursor

Beyond local events, there are opportunities to get more involved with the global Cursor community.

[**Cursor Ambassadors**\\
\\
Join us in shaping the future of development. Ambassadors empower the community that makes our ecosystem vibrant and collaborative.](https://cursor.com/ambassadors) [**Campus Leads**\\
\\
Represent Cursor at your school by teaching best practices, organizing events, and sharing Cursor with fellow students. Perfect for Boston-area students!](https://cursorai.notion.site/Cursor-on-Campus-215da74ef045808d805fd336f9a62a40)

## Join Our Community

Connect with other Cursor users in Boston. Share tips, ask questions, and stay updated on upcoming events.

[Join Cursor Boston Discord](https://discord.gg/Wsncg8YYqc)

## Get in Touch

Have questions, ideas, or want to collaborate? We'd love to hear from you.

[hello@cursorboston.com](mailto:hello@cursorboston.com) [Subscribe on Luma](https://lu.ma/cursor-boston)

pages.title: About Cursor & Our Affiliation | Cursor Boston
pages.description: Learn about Cursor, the AI-powered code editor, and how Cursor Boston connects to the global Cursor community through the Ambassadors program.
pages.content:
[Skip to main content](https://www.cursorboston.com/about-cursor#main-content)

# About Cursor & Our Affiliation

Understanding the relationship between Cursor Boston and Cursor, the AI-powered code editor

## What is Cursor?

[Cursor](https://cursor.com/) is an AI-powered code editor built on Visual Studio Code. It integrates cutting-edge AI models directly into the coding environment, providing developers with an intelligent pair programmer that understands their codebase.

Cursor is developed by **Anysphere Inc.**, a company focused on building AI-first development tools. The editor has gained significant popularity among developers for its ability to accelerate coding workflows through AI assistance.

Key features include intelligent code completion, natural language code editing, codebase-aware chat, and multi-file editing capabilities powered by frontier AI models.

### Key Facts

- Built on VS Code foundation
- Integrates Claude, GPT-4, and other AI models
- Developed by Anysphere Inc.
- Growing global developer community

[Visit cursor.com](https://cursor.com/)

## The Cursor Ambassadors Program

The [Cursor Ambassadors Program](https://cursor.com/ambassadors) is an official initiative by Cursor to empower community leaders who help make the Cursor ecosystem vibrant and collaborative. Ambassadors are passionate developers who volunteer their time to support and grow local Cursor communities around the world.

### What Ambassadors Do

- 1.Help fellow developers troubleshoot and optimize their Cursor workflow
- 2.Host community meetups, hackathons, and workshops
- 3.Share expertise and insights about AI-powered development
- 4.Provide direct feedback to the Cursor team to shape the product's future

### Ambassador Benefits

- Special badge recognition in Cursor forums
- Funding support for community meetups and events
- Direct communication channel with the Cursor team
- Connection with a global network of community leaders

### Other Community Programs

In addition to the Ambassadors program, Cursor also runs a **Campus Leads** program for students at universities. Campus Leads are representatives at schools who teach best practices, organize campus events, and share Cursor with their academic communities.

[Learn more about Cursor Community programs](https://cursor.com/community)

![Cursor Boston](https://www.cursorboston.com/_next/image?url=%2Fcursor-boston-logo.png&w=3840&q=75&dpl=dpl_3WpifsZ9mtsAJfFmEz8RarpCmtuK)

## Cursor Boston's Relationship with Cursor

### Our Status

Cursor Boston is led by members of the **Cursor Ambassadors Program**. This means we are officially recognized community leaders who have been vetted and supported by Cursor to organize local community activities in the Boston area.

### What This Means

- **Official Recognition:** We are part of Cursor's official community network and have permission to use the Cursor name
- **Support:** We receive support from Cursor to help fund and organize community events
- **Feedback Channel:** We have direct communication with the Cursor team and can relay community feedback

### What This Does NOT Mean

- **Not Employees:** We are volunteers, not employees or contractors of Cursor or Anysphere Inc.
- **Not Official Representatives:** Our views and opinions do not represent Cursor's official positions
- **Independent Operations:** Our day-to-day operations, content, and event decisions are made independently

## About Our Partners & Sponsors

Cursor Boston collaborates with various venues, sponsors, and community partners to bring events and resources to our members. It's important to understand the nature of these relationships:

- Event hosts and venue partners are **supporters of the Cursor Boston community**, not official partners of Cursor the company
- Sponsorships and partnerships with Cursor Boston **do not imply** a business relationship with Cursor or Anysphere Inc.
- Partner logos on our platform indicate support for **our local community**, not an endorsement by Cursor

## Trademark Notice

"Cursor" is a trademark of Anysphere Inc. Our use of the Cursor name in "Cursor Boston" is to identify our community of Cursor users in the Boston area.

This use is permitted through our participation in the Cursor Ambassadors Program. This does not imply ownership, endorsement, or official affiliation beyond the ambassadors program relationship described above.

## Get Involved

Whether you're interested in joining our local community or learning more about Cursor's global programs, here are some ways to get started:

[Join Cursor Boston Discord](https://discord.gg/Wsncg8YYqc) [Apply to be an Ambassador](https://cursor.com/ambassadors) [Explore Global Community](https://cursor.com/community)

Questions about our relationship with Cursor? [Contact us](mailto:hello@cursorboston.com)

See also: [Legal Disclaimer](https://www.cursorboston.com/disclaimer) \| [About Cursor Boston](https://www.cursorboston.com/about)

Join our community of developers, founders, and students exploring AI-powered development.

# Hackathons

Monthly virtual hackathons and in-person events. Form teams of three, build with Cursor, and submit your project.

[Find a team](https://www.cursorboston.com/hackathons/pool) [Teams](https://www.cursorboston.com/hackathons/teams) [My team](https://www.cursorboston.com/hackathons/team)

## Featured in-person hackathon

### Cursor Boston Hack-a-Sprint

Inaugural in-person sprint — 50 builders, solo competition, co-hosted with inkbox.ai & Genesis Fund

First-ever Cursor Boston Hack-a-Sprint: 50 developers, fast solo competition, fully fed and watered. $50 in Cursor Credits for every selected participant, $1,200 prize pool (six $200 spots). Monday April 13 in Back Bay — register on Luma; approval required.

When:Monday, April 13, 2026 • 4:00 PM – 8:00 PM ET

Where:Back Bay (exact address after approval)

Registration is approval-required on [Luma](https://luma.com/uixo8hl6). You may be asked to verify token ownership with your wallet during checkout.

#### Perks & prizes

- $50 Cursor credits (guaranteed for every participant)
- $1,200 prize pool (six $200 winning spots)
- Food and drinks

#### Schedule

- 4:00 PM — Meet, greet, and get set up. Arrive, connect with fellow developers, and get ready to build
- 4:30 PM – 7:00 PM — Hack-a-Sprint. The main build period—code, compete, and ship
- 7:00 PM – 8:00 PM — Scoring and Judging. Results calculated by participants, judges, and AI

#### How spots are prioritized

1. Open source: merged PRs to [cursor-boston on GitHub](https://github.com/rogerSuperBuilderAlpha/cursor-boston)
2. Completed profile on cursorboston.com
3. Member of the Cursor Boston Discord
4. [Website signup](https://www.cursorboston.com/hackathons/hack-a-sprint-2026/signup) ranks you by PR count (ties broken by earlier signup)
5. Luma registration (approval required)

## Virtual Hackathon – April 2026

Runs from the 1st through the last day of the month. You must be on a team of exactly 3 to participate. One team member registers the project GitHub repo to start; at the end of the month you submit and lock. Commits after the 1st of the next month (Boston time) disqualify.

Current period ends April 30, 2026.

## In-person hackathons

We run in-person hackathons at select events. Same team rules apply: teams of 3, form in advance or at the event.

### Cursor Boston Hack-a-Sprint

Inaugural in-person sprint — 50 builders, solo competition, co-hosted with inkbox.ai & Genesis Fund

First-ever Cursor Boston Hack-a-Sprint: 50 developers, fast solo competition, fully fed and watered. $50 in Cursor Credits for every selected participant, $1,200 prize pool (six $200 spots). Monday April 13 in Back Bay — register on Luma; approval required.

Featured

## Sponsor or host

Interested in supporting our community? Get in touch to sponsor prizes for the monthly virtual hackathon or to sponsor and host an in-person hackathon.

Email [hello@cursorboston.com](mailto:hello@cursorboston.com) to sponsor prizes for the monthly hackathon or to discuss sponsoring and hosting an in-person hackathon.

## Rules at a glance

- Teams must have exactly 3 members.
- To join the pool you need a public profile with GitHub and Discord connected (and Discord visible).
- You can only be on one team per hackathon.
- You can leave a team; if the team had already registered a repo, the team is disqualified and you cannot join another team until next month.
- Virtual: repo must be public and created during the hackathon month.
- Virtual: submit by end of month; no commits after the 1st of the next month (Boston time).

## How to Contribute

3

### Build It

Posted 2026-02-06

We're currently looking for a CTO/Co-Founder to join us in the journey. As we're raising and with 2026 being a major year for sports in the US (World Cup), our goal is to ship fast. We are offering an equity based role + additional compensation after raising.

#### About BeatM

We're building BeatM, a social native daily fantasy sports platform. From real time game chats, interest based communities, to multiplayer competitions and tournaments, our goal is to immerse fans through social experiences. We launched our MVP 2 months ago, spoke to over 250+ sports fans and experts to validate our idea, as well as our advisors from Fanatics, MGM, IGN, and Wynn Casinos.

Harry·COO

Senior @ Bentley with a background in marketing and fintech. Previously built two of North America's largest student nonprofits, before exiting as a freshman in college. Previously worked with the Boston Celtics, crypto, and led marketing for a NYC crypto startup through their Series A. Harry currently advises startups, nonprofit boards, and leads growth for various teams.

Nico·CEO

Previously began career in SMMAs after graduating high school, before becoming the youngest sales manager at a Swiss AI startup. Nico is from Germany and has come to Boston full time.

# Privacy Policy

Last updated: January 2026

## Introduction

Cursor Boston ("we," "our," or "us") is committed to protecting your privacy. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, disclose, and safeguard your information when you visit our website and use our services.

## Information We Collect

### Information You Provide

- Account information (name, email address) when you create an account
- Profile information (bio, social links, profile photo) that you choose to add
- Event registration information when you sign up for events
- Talk and event submissions
- Communications when you contact us or participate in community discussions

### Information Collected Automatically

- Log data (IP address, browser type, pages visited)
- Device information
- Cookies and similar tracking technologies

### Third-Party Services

When you connect third-party accounts (Discord, GitHub), we receive limited information from those services to enhance your experience.

## How We Use Your Information

- To provide and maintain our services
- To process event registrations and submissions
- To communicate with you about events and community updates
- To display your public profile to other community members (if you opt in)
- To improve our website and services
- To comply with legal obligations

## Data Sharing

We do not sell your personal information. We may share information with:

- Service providers who help us operate our platform (Firebase, hosting providers)
- Event hosts and partners, only as necessary for event coordination
- Law enforcement when required by law

## Data Security

We implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect your personal information. However, no method of transmission over the Internet is 100% secure.

## Your Rights

You have the right to:

- Access your personal information
- Correct inaccurate information
- Delete your account and associated data
- Control your profile visibility settings
- Disconnect third-party accounts at any time

## Cookies

We use cookies to maintain your session, remember preferences, and analyze site usage. See our [Cookie Policy](https://www.cursorboston.com/cookies) for more details.

## Children's Privacy

Our services are not intended for individuals under the age of 13. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children under 13.

## Changes to This Policy

We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. We will notify you by posting the new Privacy Policy on this page and updating the "Last updated" date.

## Contact Us

Questions about this Privacy Policy? Contact us at [hello@cursorboston.com](mailto:hello@cursorboston.com)

# Cookie Policy

Last updated: January 2026

## What Are Cookies

Cookies are small text files stored on your device when you visit a website. They help make websites work more efficiently and provide information to website owners.

## How We Use Cookies

### Essential Cookies

- **Authentication:** Keep you logged in
- **Security:** Protect against security threats
- **Preferences:** Remember your settings

### Analytics Cookies

- **Firebase Analytics:** Collect anonymous usage data
- **Performance monitoring:** Identify technical issues

### Third-Party Cookies

- **Firebase:** Authentication and database services
- **Luma:** Event registration widgets
- **Discord/GitHub:** OAuth account connections

## Managing Cookies

Most browsers allow you to:

- See and delete cookies individually
- Block third-party cookies
- Block all cookies
- Delete cookies when you close the browser

Note: Blocking all cookies may impact functionality, especially login features.

## Browser Settings

- [Google Chrome](https://support.google.com/chrome/answer/95647)
- [Mozilla Firefox](https://support.mozilla.org/kb/cookies-information-websites-store-on-your-computer)
- [Safari](https://support.apple.com/guide/safari/manage-cookies-sfri11471/mac)
- [Microsoft Edge](https://support.microsoft.com/microsoft-edge/delete-cookies-in-microsoft-edge)

## Contact Us

Questions about cookies? Contact us at [hello@cursorboston.com](mailto:hello@cursorboston.com)
